summer, in time to display the
scenic beauty of Spokane surround
ings to the thousands of visitors
who will pass through Spokane on
their way to or from the Seattle
exposition.


The completion of the proposed
drive will give the city a continu-
Gus boulevard ON the north and
east sides of the river from Monroe
street to Paramount cemetery.


The movement for similar
boulevard along the Hangman
creek bluff is also we'll under way
and will be Incorporated into the
boulevard system of the city as
soon as all the land in the south
west end of the city is plated and
opened for settlement


The park board hopes in the
course of five years to have a sys-
tenn of boulevard driveways around
the river and hill bluffs of the city
20 miles in length. No city in the
country presents better opportuni-
ties for development of this kind
that Spokane.


The park board will employ the
Olmstead Bros, well known land
scape gardeners. to lay out the new
boulevard.
